campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax amounts, and the council publicly discussed and voted on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased or adjusted
Annual tax rate update for companies doing business in Campbell
Finance department recommendations on new tax amounts
City council vote on whether to approve the changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the usual first formal reading to speed up the process. They directed staff to get the ordinance ready for a final vote at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular meeting
First reading waived to expedite the approval process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, where they approved meeting minutes from earlier in the month, reviewed the city's finances for February, and authorized the Town Manager to sign several construction and professional service contracts. The council also worked on approving improvements to town infrastructure and related planning documents.
keyTopics: Approved financial reports showing the city's money and investments through February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with multiple companies, including QLM, Inc.
Moved forward on town infrastructure improvements and related planning
Approved professional service agreements for a three-year period

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 27, 2026
san-jose.summary: The City Council reviewed progress reports on several major city initiatives, including updates on planning projects happening across San José, the Team San José economic development efforts, and big events scheduled for 2026. They also discussed the city's economic strategy and how it's being implemented this year.
keyTopics: Citywide planning projects and development activities
Team San José's economic development progress
2026 major events scheduled for the city
Annual economic strategy implementation updates

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 27, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 27, 2026 that included both in-person and virtual attendance options. Residents were able to participate remotely via Zoom or phone call to watch and comment on city business.
keyTopics: Meeting held with hybrid in-person and virtual participation option
Public comment instructions for Zoom participants (use 'raise hand' feature)
Public comment instructions for phone participants (dial *9 to raise hand, *6 to unmute)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 27, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 27, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to watch and comment on the proceedings both in person and online.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment opportunity for residents
Online and in-person participation options

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an Audit Committee meeting where residents were given a chance to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council approved meeting minutes from January and reviewed how well the city's retirement and health benefits investment accounts performed in the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period on any city matters
Approval of January 26 Audit Committee meeting minutes
Q1 2026 performance review of city retirement and health benefits investments

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters that will run through 2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighter contract agreement approved through 2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve several financial and development matters for the upcoming fiscal year. The council reviewed tax responsibilities, development fees, and user charges for city services, while also discussing regional efforts to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development fees and charges approval
User fees for city services updates
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming council calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to take another look at a tree removal permit that was already approved for 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to reconsider the decision to allow cutting down a protected tree on that property.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request for Planning Director to review an already-approved permit
Dispute over whether a tree should be cut down
